name: Holiday Anise Cookie
recipeInstructions:
------------------------
DECORATION
1 tablespoon Sugar
1 teaspoon Ground cinnamon
1. Thoroughly stir together flour, anise, baking powder, and salt in a bowl. Set aside.
2. Using a small sharp knife, peel a thin layer of zest (without the white membrane) from the entire lemon.
3. Combine lemon zest and sugar in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Process until the zest is very finely minced.
4. Add shortening and butter to the processor and process for one minute.
5. Add egg yolk and vanilla, and process for 20 seconds longer.
6. Add dry ingredients and continue processing just until they are blended.
7. Divide dough in half and place each portion between large sheets of waxed paper. Roll out to 1/8 inch thick.
8. Check the underside of the dough for any creases. Slide the dough onto a large tray or baking sheet and refrigerate for 15 minutes, or until the dough is chilled and slightly firm.
9. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
10. Lightly grease several baking sheets.
11. Work with one half of the dough at a time. Turn the dough over and peel off the sheet of wax paper from the dough. Replace the paper loosely.
12. Turn the dough right side up. Peel off and discard the top sheet of wax paper.
13. Using assorted 2-1/4 to 3-inch cookie cutters, cut out cookies.
14. Place the cookies in the oven and bake for 8-10 minutes or until just brown around the edges. Remove from the oven and let stand for 3 minutes.
15. Transfer cookies to wire racks and let cool completely.
16. Sprinkle cookies with the decoration mixture.
Makes 35 to 40 cookies.
